sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me look at the white (W) objects more carefully. There are two white objects (id=1 and id=7) with 19 pixels each. They might be targets or indicators. Let me check their positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check white object positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"White: rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check black object positions
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'B':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Black: rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
